channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters playoff to complete the career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Rory McIlroy turned another major collapse into his grandest moment of all, hitting a wedge into 3 feet for birdie in a sudden-death playoff Sunday to finally win the Masters and take his place in golf history as the sixth player to claim the career Grand Slam.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palou finally loses as IndyCar champ's 2-race winning streak snapped by Kirkwood in Long Beach
item.description: LONG BEACH, Calif. (AP) — Kyle Kirkwood showed that Alex Palou can be beat this IndyCar season and returned Andretti Global to the top of the podium with his second career victory at the Grand Prix of Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US downs defending champion Canada 2-1 to stay perfect at women’s hockey worlds
item.description: CESKE BUDEJOVICE, Czech Republic (AP) — The United States downed defending champion Canada 2-1 at the women’s hockey world championship on Sunday in a matchup of the game’s global powers. Lee Stecklein and Megan Keller scored for the Americans who made it three wins from three in Group A. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In Year 20, the Spurs' Chris Paul starts all 82 games. And he made some history in the process
item.description: In Year 20, Chris Paul started all 82. And he made some NBA history in the process. Paul was in the San Antonio Spurs' starting lineup for their season finale on Sunday, making him the first player to ever make 82 starts this deep into a career.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Celtics star Jaylen Brown received injections in right knee, coach Joe Mazzulla says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla on Sunday confirmed a report that Jaylen Brown received injections during the week to help his troublesome right knee that’s limited his playing time the past month. It was first reported by ESPN that Brown received pain management injections.
item.dc:creator: KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blazers sign coach Chauncey Billups to a multi-year extension
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Coach Chauncey Billups has signed a multi-year contract extension with the Portland Trail Blazers. The Blazers did not release terms of the deal, announced before the regular-season finale against the Lakers on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NASCAR drivers Sheldon Creed, Brennan Poole involved in serious crash at Bristol
item.description: Sheldon Creed and Brennan Poole were involved in a serious crash during the NASCAR Xfinity Series race at Bristol Motor Speedway on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bryson DeChambeau is trying to chase down Rory McIlroy again in a major. He has 18 holes to do it
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Bryson DeChambeau finds himself trying to chase down Rory McIlroy in another major championship. This time, he has 18 holes to do it. The two-time and reigning U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gunner Stockton strengthens hold on Georgia starting quarterback job with strong spring practice
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — Carson Beck's decision to transfer to Miami opened the door for Gunner Stockton to take over as Georgia's starting quarterback.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rory McIlroy chips in eagle, Bryson DeChambeau sinks deep birdie to start Masters third round with fireworks
item.description: Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau made Masters fireworks during the third round at Augusta National on Saturday, burying eagles and birdies to sit at the top of the leaderboard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blackhawks score 4 3rd-period goals for 5-2 win over Bruins
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Nick Foligno scored a pair of goals, Arvid Soderblom made 31 saves and the Chicago Blackhawks beat the Boston Bruins 5-2 on Thursday night. Ryan Donato, Tyler Bertuzzi and Kevin Korchinski also had goals for the Blackhawks, who scored four goals in third period to pull away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: College athletes and San Diego students with disabilities team up for day of sports
item.description: The Inclusive Sports Foundation has provided children with special needs the opportunity to play sports with college athletes and recently stopped by the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Julian Del Gaudio, Fox 5

item.title: Fans impressed with new Bees Ballpark
item.description: SOUTH JORDAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Welcome to The Ballpark. The state of Utah has not had a brand new stadium since 2008 when Real Salt Lake opened Rio Tinto Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 49ers star’s high school retired his jersey
item.description: (KRON) – For the first time, a Colorado high school has retired a jersey for one of its former students. That alum was none other than San Francisco 49ers star Christian McCaffrey. Valor Christian High School recently retired McCaffrey’s No.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tickets to the Salt Lake Bees new stadium will cost you more than they used to
item.description: The Salt Lake Bees are playing their first game at the new ballpark in South Jordan tomorrow, though fans of the team might notice that prices are a little different than Smith's Ballpark.
item.dc:creator: Sorina Trauntvein, ABC 4

item.title: Johnny Gaudreau's wife Meredith announces birth of couple's third child
item.description: Johnny and his brother were killed in New Jersey when they were riding bicycles and struck from behind by a suspected drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: LA Dodgers visit White House to celebrate World Series win
item.description: Team president Stan Kasten said all the players wanted to attend, according to the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO Associated Press, Fox 5
